sections.title: 1) Word Order Basics
points: A common beginner sentence pattern is Subject + Verb + Object.
Hungarian word order can shift for emphasis, but starting with SVO is practical for beginners.

sections.title: 2) Articles and Nouns
points: Hungarian has two articles: "a" and "az" (both mean "the").
Use "az" before vowel sounds and "a" before consonant sounds.
There is no grammatical gender for nouns (no masculine/feminine forms).

sections.title: 3) Pronunciation Tips
points: Hungarian stress usually falls on the first syllable.
Letter pairs like "sz" sound like English "s", while "s" sounds like "sh".
Vowel length can change meaning, so pay attention to accent marks (e.g., o vs ó).

conjugationTable.title: Conjugation table: lenni (to be) — present tense
conjugationTable.subtitle: A high-frequency irregular verb worth memorizing early.
